summary: Typosquat of @solana-labs/spl-token exfiltrates host data via postinstall
details: Package name @solana-labs/spl-toke is a one-character omission of the legitimate @solana-labs/spl-token (Solana Program Library token client). The package.json declares a postinstall lifecycle hook ("postinstall": "node install.js") that runs install.js automatically on every npm install. install.js imports fs, os, http, and https, collects host identifiers via os.hostname() and process.platform, invokes execSync and curl-based command execution, and performs HTTPS POST requests carrying that data off the installer's machine. The combination of a typosquatted scope/name targeting a widely-used Solana SDK, an auto-executing postinstall script, system identity collection, shell execution, and outbound POST is a credential/host exfiltration dropper that fires automatically on default install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
